steps.prompt: Which word is the main verb?

hints.mortem: 'mortem' is a noun in the accusative meaning 'death'
hints.duci: 'duci' is a passive infinitive meaning 'to be led'

hints.to hope: 'to hope' is 'sperare' — 'oportere' means 'it is fitting/it is necessary'
hints.to cover: 'to cover' is 'operire' — 'oportere' is an impersonal verb meaning 'it is right/necessary'

reveal.gloss: it was fitting / you ought to have been

hints.Present indicative: Present would be 'oportet' — 'oportebat' with -ebat ending is imperfect
hints.Imperfect subjunctive: Imperfect subjunctive would be 'oporteret' — 'oportebat' is indicative

hints.operio, operire, operui, opertum: These are the parts of 'operire' (to cover/hide)
hints.opto, optare, optavi, optatum: These are the parts of 'optare' (to wish/choose)
hints.opprimo, opprimere, oppressi, oppressum: These are the parts of 'opprimere' (to crush/suppress)

steps.target: An unfulfilled obligation — 'you should have been … (but were not)'
choices: An unfulfilled obligation — 'you should have been … (but were not)'

hints.A present obligation: Present obligation would use 'oportet' — imperfect 'oportebat' implies the obligation was not fulfilled
hints.A future possibility: 'oportebat' looks to the past, not the future — it means 'you should have been (but weren't)'
hints.A past completed action: If completed, there would be no need for the imperfect — 'oportebat' implies the action was never done

hints.Nominative: Nominative would be 'mors' — 'mortem' with -em ending is accusative after 'ad'
hints.Dative: Dative would be 'morti' — 'mortem' is accusative after 'ad'
hints.Ablative: Ablative would be 'morte' — 'ad' governs the accusative

hints.Active — 'to lead': Active infinitive would be 'ducere' — 'duci' is the passive infinitive
hints.Deponent — 'to lead': 'ducere' is not deponent — 'duci' is a true passive infinitive
hints.Middle — 'to lead oneself': Latin does not have a middle voice — 'duci' is simply passive

steps.prompt: What is the full meaning of 'Ad mortem te duci oportebat'?
steps.target: You should long ago have been led to execution
choices: You are being led to death now
You should long ago have been led to execution
Someone will lead you to death

hints.You are being led to death now: 'oportebat' (imperfect) + 'iam pridem' (long ago) shows this should have already happened
hints.Someone will lead you to death: The imperfect 'oportebat' refers to past obligation, not future action
hints.Death is leading you away: 'te duci' means 'you to be led' — the subject being led is Catiline, not death

translation: You should long ago have been led to your death, Catiline.
